PBk11: Mars Black (Synthetic Iron Oxide)

Wavelength (nm) | Reflectance (%) | Description
----------------|-----------------|------------------------------------------
360 – 400       | 2.8% – 3.2%    | Ultraviolet: Extremely low, stable absorption.
400 – 550       | 3.2% – 3.8%    | Visible (Blue-Green): Flat, neutral black plateau.
550 – 700       | 3.8% – 5.1%    | Visible (Red): Subtle rising slope (warmth).
700 – 800       | 5.1% – 12.4%   | NIR: Signature rapid rise of Iron Oxide.

PBk9: Ivory Black (Bone Black)

Wavelength (nm) | Reflectance (%) | Description
----------------|-----------------|------------------------------------------
360 – 400       | 1.8% – 2.0%    | Ultraviolet: High absorption, very stable.
400 – 700       | 2.0% – 2.2%    | Visible: Flat, near-perfect neutral absorption.
700 – 750       | 2.2% – 2.3%    | Red Edge: Minimal rising slope compared to PBk11.
750 – 800       | 2.3% – 2.5%    | NIR: Remains highly absorptive (non-reflective).

PB15:1: Winsor Blue (Red Shade) / Phthalo Blue (Alpha Modification)

Wavelength (nm) | Reflectance (%) | Description
----------------|-----------------|------------------------------------------
360 – 410       | 4.5% – 16.5%   | UV/Violet: Moderate rise, providing the "red" cast.
440 – 470       | 28.0% – 33.5%  | Visible (Blue): Primary reflectance plateau.
580 – 660       | 1.2% – 1.8%    | Visible (Orange/Red): Deepest absorption valley.
700 – 800       | 12.0% – 86.0%  | NIR: Steep organic rise to high transparency.

PBr7: Burnt Umber (Calcined Natural Earth)

Wavelength (nm) | Reflectance (%) | Description
----------------|-----------------|------------------------------------------
360 – 420       | 2.5% – 3.8%    | UV/Violet: Very deep absorption; nearly black base.
450 – 550       | 4.0% – 6.5%    | Visible (Blue/Green): Muted, slow rising slope.
550 – 700       | 6.5% – 14.5%   | Visible (Yellow/Red): Warm, dark "earth" rise.
700 – 800       | 14.5% – 42.0%  | NIR: Signature iron oxide transparency climb.

PO71: Transparent Pyrrole Orange (DPP Orange)

Wavelength (nm) | Reflectance (%) | Description
----------------|-----------------|------------------------------------------
360 – 480       | 1.2% – 2.8%    | UV/Blue: Deep, total absorption (The "Step").
500 – 580       | 2.8% – 52.0%   | Visible (Green/Yellow): Extremely sharp rising edge.
600 – 700       | 75.0% – 88.5%  | Visible (Orange/Red): High reflectance plateau.
700 – 800       | 88.5% – 94.0%  | NIR: Extreme transparency; reaches maximum reflectance.

PV23: Winsor Violet (Dioxazine Violet)

Wavelength (nm) | Reflectance (%) | Description
----------------|-----------------|------------------------------------------
360 – 400       | 3.2% – 5.5%    | UV/Violet: Very low, rising toward blue peak.
430 – 450       | 7.8% – 9.2%    | Visible (Violet): Primary reflectance peak (muted).
500 – 680       | 0.5% – 1.8%    | Visible (Green/Red): Total absorption; near-black.
700 – 800       | 15.0% – 88.0%  | NIR: Extreme organic rise to high transparency.

PV19: Permanent Carmine (Quinacridone Carmine)

Wavelength (nm) | Reflectance (%) | Description
----------------|-----------------|------------------------------------------
360 – 420       | 5.8% – 12.5%   | UV/Violet: Moderate reflectance; cooler than Cadmiums.
520 – 570       | 1.5% – 2.8%    | Visible (Green/Yellow): Deepest "W" absorption valley.
620 – 700       | 35.0% – 78.0%  | Visible (Red): Steep, transparent reflectance rise.
700 – 800       | 78.0% – 94.0%  | NIR: Extreme transparency; maximum reflectance plateau.
